tldr: Baremetrics is SaaS / subscription metrics derived primarily from Stripe — not a like-for-like Amplitude replacement. Use it if your real need is MRR, churn and revenue analytics, not behavioural product analytics.
why_switch: Focused SaaS revenue, MRR, churn dashboards
Less engineering overhead — no event instrumentation
export_method: Use Amplitude's account settings to download chart configurations and cohorts as CSV. Raw event data can be pulled through the Export API or via the warehouse-native connection if you set one up (Snowflake/BigQuery sync). Treat the event archive as cold storage — Baremetrics will not ingest it. Document your event schema, identify-call shape, and any computed properties for future reference. Export user cohorts to CSV so you can re-create equivalent customer segments in Baremetrics manually if needed. Save chart screenshots for institutional knowledge.
import_method: In Baremetrics, the primary onboarding step is connecting your billing provider (Stripe, Braintree, Recurly, Chargebee, etc.) via the dashboard. Once connected, MRR, churn, LTV, ARR and cohort revenue charts populate automatically — no SDK install or event tagging needed. If you also want simple page or signup tracking, you'll need a separate tool; Baremetrics is revenue-centric. Recreate any customer segments using the customers/properties available from your billing source. Invite teammates and configure Slack / email digests. Plan a one-month overlap with Amplitude so finance can reconcile metrics before cancelling.
gotchas: Not a 1:1 replacement — behavioural analytics are lost, only revenue remains
Amplitude annual event commit does not pro-rate mid-cycle
Baremetrics depends on a clean billing source — messy Stripe data = messy charts
Funnels, retention, and product event analysis must move to a different tool
Historical raw events are not importable into Baremetrics

email.subject: Account export and wind-down — Amplitude
email.body:
Hi Amplitude team,

We are restructuring our analytics stack and will wind down our Amplitude account over the next 14 days. To do this cleanly, could you help with:
1. Confirming the best path to export raw events, cohorts, and chart definitions via the API or warehouse sync.
2. Sharing the end date of our current annual event-volume commitment so we can avoid overlap charges.
3. Letting us know if any data is purged immediately on cancellation versus retained for a grace period.

We plan to run a short parallel period for reconciliation before final cancellation. Please let me know if anything needs to be initiated from your side.

Thanks,
Your customer
